summary: Phase B (FFT phase rand HF + magnitude jitter + micro time-warping) + Phase C (short-IR convolution + stereo M/S decorrelation) + MP3 export (@breezystack/lamejs) + Random Seed mode all PASSED end-to-end on Chromium with zero console errors and zero /api/* calls. Tested with stereo 4s 440Hz WAV (705KB).
critical_code_review_comments: audioProcessor.js processBuffer chains FFT pre-pass + 2 OfflineAudioContext renders cleanly; Stealth pipeline (~5-10s for 4s stereo input) completes without setValueCurveAtTime NotSupportedError on Chromium.
mp3Encoder.js uses Float32→Int16 conversion before lamejs Mp3Encoder — produced valid 163KB audio/mp3 blob from 4s stereo input (320kbps). Blob MIME type correctly set to 'audio/mp3'.
fftProcessing.js + buildShortRoomIR + ConvolverNode(normalize=false) + DelayNode(maxDelayTime=0.005s, delayTime=80µs) all work correctly — no FFT.js import errors, no convolver/delay node errors.
randomiseSettings produces verifiably different outputs across runs (run1 hash=4202573212/713280 bytes vs run2 hash=3744252299/710876 bytes — both size AND content differ).
Hero copy now correctly mentions 'FFT phase randomization · resample chain · pitch+time shift · convolution · M/S decorrelation · pink noise floor · strip metadata' — matches actual pipeline.
Minor cosmetic: 'FFT' string appears 4 times on the page (hero text + Phase B section badge + step 01 + step 02 description) — expected, not a bug. Phase B section badge alone is unique.
Files remain under 700 LOC threshold; clean module separation across audioProcessor / fftProcessing / mp3Encoder / wavEncoder.
seed_data_creation: Generated /tmp/t1.wav (4s 440Hz STEREO, 44.1kHz, 16-bit PCM, 705644 bytes) for stereo decorrelation testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pure static SPA at https://music-no-mark.preview.emergentagent.com (no backend). Iteration 3 added Phase B (FFT-based: phase rand HF, magnitude jitter, time-warping) + Phase C (convolution + stereo decorr) + MP3 export + Random Seed toggle. Stealth preset enables ALL 9 anti-detection toggles (4 Phase A + 3 Phase B + 2 Phase C). FFT pre-pass is heavy CPU and adds 5-10s for 4s stereo audio — use timeout >= 45s for processing waits. MP3 blob via @breezystack/lamejs produces ~160KB for 4s stereo. Random Seed verified: same input + stealth produces DIFFERENT bytes/sizes per run. Use STEREO test WAV. New test IDs: toggle-phaserand, toggle-magjitter, toggle-timewarp, toggle-convolution, toggle-stereodecorr, slider-phasehf, slider-magdb, slider-warpdepth, slider-convmix, slider-stereodelay, render-options, format-toggle, format-wav, format-mp3, toggle-random-seed.

rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no bugs found. All risky paths (FFT.js import, ConvolverNode normalize=false, DelayNode 80µs, lamejs Float32→Int16, setValueCurveAtTime) executed cleanly on Chromium.
